data.title: Global DRaaS Market to reach $46,089.9 million by 2032, growing from $16,112.2 million in 2025 - MarketsandMarkets™.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, as of January 19, 2026, data from MarketsandMarkets™ reveals significant growth in the global Disaster Recovery as a Service (DRaaS) Market. It is forecasted to increase from approximately USD 16,112.2 million in 2025 to a projected market size of USD 46,089.9 million by 2032. The substantial rise showcases the rapid expansion of the industry and underscores the increasing importance of DRaaS solutions in the market.

data.title: Acosta Group and CommerceIQ form alliance, leading in intelligent commerce with AI automation for brands.
data.description: Cutting-edge omnichannel implementation and AI-powered automation are revolutionizing brand strategies. A collaboration between Acosta Group's Connected Commerce team and CommerceIQ, announced on January 19, 2026, in Jacksonville, Florida, has resulted in unparalleled efficiency and real-time decision-making capabilities. This partnership leverages the unique abilities of both entities to provide brands with lightning-fast operations and advanced data-driven insights.

data.title: BE OPEN Regional Art competition announces winners for Western Africa region. Developed by Elena Baturina, it highlights emerging artists.
data.description: In Lugano, Switzerland on January 19, 2026, the winners of the BE OPEN Regional Art competition for emerging artists in Western Africa were announced. This competition is organized by Elena Baturina's humanitarian think-tank BE OPEN. The winning artists were selected for their exemplary work during the months of October to December.

data.title: Transparency essential for public trust in autonomous vehicles per Amy Witherite; Waymo's refusal raises accountability concerns.
data.description: In Dallas, a traffic safety expert and attorney, Amy Witherite, emphasizes the importance of transparency from companies involved in autonomous vehicles as they increasingly operate on public roads. Witherite stresses that transparency is crucial for maintaining public trust and ensuring safety. Recent reports reveal that Waymo, a prominent player in autonomous vehicles, refused to share information with the California Public Utilities Commission regarding a significant power outage and service disruption. This incident has raised concerns about accountability within the rapidly expanding autonomous vehicle industry.

data.title: Navitus attains HITRUST r2 Certification for cybersecurity, marks third consecutive certification since 2023.
data.description: In Madison, Wisconsin, Navitus, the country's foremost transparent pass-through pharmacy benefit manager (PBM), revealed that it has secured HITRUST r2 Certification for cybersecurity and information protection. This marks Navitus' third consecutive HITRUST certification, initially obtained in 2023. The Certification serves as external confirmation that Navitus has integrated robust controls in line with the highest cybersecurity standards.

data.title: SER Group rebrands to 'Doxis' & emphasizes AI focus, appoints new leaders. Exciting change for the company.
data.description: The company SER Group, based in Bonn, Germany, has officially rebranded itself as "Doxis" to align with its flagship Intelligent Content Automation platform and solutions. This change comes alongside the introduction of new senior executives in sales and AI to drive the company's growth and technological advancement. The rebranding aims to position Doxis as "The Document Intelligence Company", marking a significant milestone in its transition as a prominent provider in this sector.

data.title: Lightcurve starts fiber network construction in Selah, Washington, to serve 2,900 locations with internet speeds up to 2,000 Mbps.
data.description: In SELAH, Wash., Lightcurve, a rapidly expanding broadband provider in Washington state, has begun constructing a new fiber network in Selah. This initiative aims to increase access to high-speed, dependable internet for residents and businesses in the area. The network will cater to approximately 2,900 locations in Selah, providing symmetrical internet speeds of up to 2,000 Mbps. This strategic investment signifies a substantial upgrade to broadband infrastructure in the community.

data.title: ISG Center of Excellence in Bangalore, India, certified as Great Place To Work for the 7th year in a row.
data.description: Stamford, Connecticut - The ISG Center of Excellence located in Bangalore, India, has once again received certification from Great Place To Work® for the seventh year in a row.

data.title: RefrigiWear® enhances ComfortGuard™ workwear for better mobility with lighter insulation and improved weather protection.
data.description: In Dahlonega, Georgia, on January 19, 2026, RefrigiWear, a prominent producer of insulated industrial workwear, introduced an updated line aimed at industrial and outdoor workers. This updated line promises better mobility, lighter insulation, and improved weather protection while maintaining durability. This development provides workers with enhanced functionality in their work environments.

data.title: PacketFabric & Massed Compute introduce integrated GPUaaS & NaaS for AI, simplifying enterprise workload deployment.
data.description: In a groundbreaking move, PacketFabric and Massed Compute have introduced the pioneering integration of on-demand GPU compute and on-demand networking. This innovation is set to revolutionize the way enterprises deploy and expand AI workloads. The collaboration, announced on January 19, 2026, in Culver City, California and Seattle, aims to simplify the process and enhance scalability for businesses. PacketFabric, renowned for its Network-as-a-Service (NaaS) offerings, has joined forces with Massed Compute to deliver a seamless solution that caters to the growing demand for AI capabilities. This strategic partnership signifies a significant step forward in the industry, leveraging cutting-edge technology to meet the needs of modern enterprises.

data.title: "The Perfect Franchise debuts 'The Perfect Match' series showcasing real franchise placements. Launch on Jan. 19, 2026."
data.description: On January 19, 2026, in Shohola, Pennsylvania, The Perfect Franchise, a prominent franchise consulting company in the U.S., revealed the debut of The Perfect Match. This original video series provides a glimpse into actual franchise placements. The series showcases the process involved in matching individuals with suitable franchises.
